notes: Best by average score: loose_baseline (score 35.76, avg return -0.118%, avg expectancy -0.0495).
loose_baseline helped vs strict_full (Δscore 15.76, Δexpectancy -0.0495, Δtrades 59.6) mostly by changing turnover.
strict_no_long_entry_led was neutral vs strict_full (Δscore 0.0, Δexpectancy 0.0, Δtrades 0.0) without much turnover change.
strict_long_entry_legacy was neutral vs strict_full (Δscore 0.0, Δexpectancy 0.0, Δtrades 0.0) without much turnover change.

limitations: This runner compares backtest behavior on the CSV set you feed it; it does not by itself discover markets.
LLM classifier and market-family filtering are selection-layer tools, so they are not cleanly isolatable from raw CSV-only backtests in the same way as entry/exit/gating toggles.
Legacy long_entry and legacy strict exits are intentionally old-like approximations for comparison, not exact historical reproductions.
